libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that exercise Guice AOP, interceptors, or CGLIB-enhanced classes, configure both the test JVM and native-image build with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`. Prefer public Guice APIs for coverage. If directly touching relocated Guice internals, note that this artifact names many shaded internal classes with a leading `$`, for example `com.google.inject.internal.util.$MapMaker` and `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.$MethodProxy`; imports without the `$` will not compile for `2.9.1`.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Direct tests of `com.google.inject.internal.*` are brittle across Sisu Guice versions and caused the reported javac failure.
Plain injector/binding coverage works without extra setup, but AOP/interceptor coverage fails at runtime on JDK 25 without the `java.lang` open.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The default artifact already carries the needed Guice internals and transitive dependencies; no extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed, but meaningful AOP/CGLIB coverage requires opening java.lang on modern JDKs.
